title: Human Islet Cryopreservation Version 2.0
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fnc3","text":"This protocol details the cryopreservation of human islets, as performed by the Alberta Diabetes Institute IsletCore. Human islets cryopreserved using this method have been found to retain viability and function after 20 years of cryogenic biobanking.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fqrh9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2gdk7","text":"Manning Fox JE, Lyon J, Dai XQ, Wright RC, Hayward J, van de Bunt M, Kin  T, Shapiro AMJ, McCarthy MI, Gloyn AL, Ungrin MD, Lakey JR, Kneteman  NM, Warnock GL, Korbutt GS, Rajotte RV, MacDonald PE (2015) Human islet function following 20 years of cryogenic biobanking. Diabetologia, 58(7): 1503-1512.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":269,"length":14},{"key":1,"offset":283,"length":16}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"EA76D38EC17E4865A55E98FE0938089D","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/s00125-015-3598-4"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"1D89C4197F23436194348688EAB3E83C","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/s00125-015-3598-4"}}}}

steps: %s %s A B C D E 1 M199 (10L) Concentration Weight/Volume Supplier Catalogue # 2 M199 powder 9.41g/L 1 bottle Mediatech-Corning 90050PB 3 3 NaHCO3 26 mM 22.0g Fisher Scientific S233-500 4 HEPES 10 mM 23.83g Fisher Scientific BP310-1 5 Penicillin/Streptomycin 20,000 U/ml penicillin and 20,000 μg/ml streptomycin 50ml Lonza 09-757F Preparation Prepare the M199 solution using the above Media Preparation table above: Dispense 9 L of Milli-Q (18mΩ) water in to a carboy Store overnight at 4 °C to allow to come to temperature. Using a stirrer add the M199 media powder to the water and allow to mix into solution. Add the powdered supplements and Penicillin/Streptomycin to the appropriate media based on the above table and allow to stir into solution. Stir the solution for 00:30:00 Store the prepared solution overnight at 4 °C to allow all powders to go into solution Stir the solution for 00:30:00 Calibrate the pH meter using the pH control buffers Adjust the pH level of M199 solution to pH 7.4 using the NaOH and/or HCl. Bring to volume of 10 L with the appropriate amount of Milli-Q water (18mΩ). Filter into 1 L bottles with a 0.22µm nitrocellulose filter.
Pre-cool FTS bath and set temperature at -7.4 °C starting temp approximately 02:00:00 prior beginning the cryopreservation.

Label cryopreservation tubes using Brady™ FREEZERBONDZ™ Polyester Thermal Transfer Printer Labels (Fisher Scientific Cat #22-500521). Include the following information. Internal Identifier number (Rxxx) Number of islet equivalents (IEQ) per tube Cryoprotectant (DMSO) Cryopreservation date Tube number
Aliquot islets into the cryopreservation tubes with a maximum of 25,000 IEQ per cryopreservation tube.
Centrifuge all tubes 280 x g, 4 °C, 1 minute . After centrifugation, remove the supernatant.
At room temperature, add 1 mL of freeze M199 to each tube and suspend the islets. 22 °C
At room temperature, add 500 µL 2M DMSO and start timer. 22 °C 00:00:00
At 5 minutes add an additional 500 µL 2M DMSO. 22 °C
At 45 minutes transfer all tubes to a rack in an ice bath. 0 °C, ice bath
At 55 minutes transfer all tubes to the pre-chilled FTS bath (-7.5°C) -7.5 °C, Seeding bath

At 65 minutes nucleate* all tubes to release the latent heat of fusion -7.5 °C, Nucleation *Nucleation is necessary to remove the latent heat of fusion. At this point the tissue suspension is "super-cooled". This refers to the fact that the suspension is liquid despite being at -7.5°C. Nucleation is achieved by submersing each tube for 2 seconds in liquid nitrogen and then applying a mechanical force (firmly hit the tube against a solid object). If nucleation is successful the solution will appear to be a "slush". Immediately after nucleation return each tube back to the seeding bath.
At 85 minutes begin the FTS bath rate cooling procedure at 2.5°C/min -7.5 °C, cool down by 2.5°C/min
Once the FTS bath reaches -40 °C all tubes can be transferred to liquid nitrogen

Once the cryopreservation protocol is complete and the tubes have been transferred to liquid nitrogen (-190°C), the tubes can be transferred to long term storage in a liquid nitrogen vapour phase. This vapour phase can be achieved by using a Chart MVE 1500 Series LN 2 storage tank or similar system. -190 °C, store in LN2 vapour phase Human islets cryopreserved in this manner can be successfully thawed using the Thawing Cryopreserved Human Islets protocol.

Freeze M199: Prepare Freeze M199 solution as follows: Add 100 mL heat inactivated FBS (HyClone™ Fetal Bovine Serum (Canada), Characterized - Fisher cat# SH3039603) to 900 mL M199 media. Store at 4 °C . Warm to room temperature prior to use. Heat inactivation of FBS Thaw serum and aliquot into labelled 50ml tubes. If serum was thawed in a refrigerator allow serum to come to room temperature prior to placing in water bath. Fill the water-bath with sufficient water so that the tubes may be submersed to the level of the serum. Set water-bath temperature to maintain the product at 56 °C . Once 56 °C is reached, place the tubes in the water-bath for 00:30:00 . After 30 minutes immediately remove the tubes from the water bath. Store the tubes of heat inactivated FBS at -20 °C .

3M DMSO: Add Freeze M199 to 11.7 g, DMSO of DMSO (Fisher Scientific - Cat# BP231)for a total volume of 50ml. Filter sterilize through a syringe filter (Nylon 0.2µm pore) into a sterile glass media bottle. Store at 4 °C . Warm to room temperature prior to use.

2M DMSO: Add Freeze M199 to 7.8 g, DMSO (Fisher Scientific - Cat# BP231)for a total volume of 50ml. Filter sterilize through a syringe filter (Nylon 0.2µm pore) into a sterile glass media bottle Store at 4 °C . Warm to room temperature prior to use.
creator: James Lyon
